authoritative_sources.title: Food Code 2022
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA publishes the Food Code as the national model used by retail food regulators. It is authoritative for retail food establishment controls and fresh-department terminology such as TCS food, hot and cold holding, date marking, shellstock tags, cleaning, sanitization, and active managerial control.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: fresh-department readiness, food-handling evidence, hold or rework boundary review
used_for: TCS food, temperature, date-marking, and shellstock evidence expectations
fresh-case and prep-room readiness terminology
boundary between execution evidence review and food-safety release authority

authoritative_sources.title: Retail & Food Service HACCP
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's retail HACCP materials are public federal guidance for applying food-safety management principles in retail and food-service operations. They are authoritative for requiring process evidence and escalation when fresh-department controls are incomplete.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: active managerial control and process-evidence review
used_for: workflow stages for receiving, prep, holding, display, and corrective action routing
evidence needs for logs, checks, and active managerial control
refusal when process controls are missing

authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule: Requirements for Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA administers FSMA food traceability requirements. The rule is authoritative for traceability lot code and critical tracking event concepts that matter when fresh-department products, recalls, pull-and-hold actions, or customer complaints depend on lot-level evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: lot, traceability, recall, and customer-impact escalation
used_for: lot and traceability evidence
recall and pull-and-hold blocker handling
domain failure modes tied to missing traceability records

authoritative_sources.title: Retail Exemptions for Meat and Poultry Products
authoritative_sources.publisher: USDA Food Safety and Inspection Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FSIS is the federal meat and poultry inspection authority. Its retail-exemption guidance defines the boundary between ordinary retail meat-department operations and activities that require different inspection or compliance handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: meat and poultry department scope, boundary, and escalation review
used_for: meat and poultry retail-boundary checks
conditions requiring food-safety or compliance escalation
refusal of requests to approve operations beyond lane authority

authoritative_sources.title: Recordkeeping Requirements for Retail Stores That Grind Raw Beef Products
authoritative_sources.publisher: USDA Food Safety and Inspection Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FSIS requires certain retail stores that grind raw beef to keep records supporting traceback. It is authoritative for treating missing grind-log, supplier, or lot evidence as a blocker in meat-department execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: meat department traceability and completion evidence review
used_for: grind-log and supplier-lot evidence expectations
traceback-sensitive blocker handling
completion refusal when required meat records are absent

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Handbook 130 - Uniform Laws and Regulations in the Areas of Legal Metrology and Fuel Quality
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST Office of Weights and Measures publishes the public baseline used by jurisdictions for method-of-sale, unit pricing, price verification, and package labeling controls. It is authoritative for grocery fresh price, unit-price, random-weight, and scale-label conflict handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: pricing, method-of-sale, random-weight, unit-price, and scale-label review
used_for: PLU, random-weight, scale-label, and unit-price evidence expectations
shelf, scale, and POS price conflict handling
boundary against unauthorized price or markdown overrides

authoritative_sources.title: Selling on the Internet: Prompt Delivery Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the federal consumer-protection authority for unfair or deceptive commerce practices. Its online order guidance is authoritative for promised delivery timing, delay notices, cancellation, and prompt refunds when online grocery fresh-order commitments cannot be met.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: e-commerce grocery order, pickup, delay, cancellation, and refund exception handling
used_for: online order and pickup commitment boundaries
order mismatch and prompt-refund escalation
customer-impact scenario design

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 General Specifications Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized global standards body for retail identifiers and barcode data. Its standards are authoritative for connecting GTIN, UPC, barcode, and location data across fresh labels, inventory, orders, and traceability records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: item identity, scale-label, order, inventory, and traceability record linkage
used_for: GTIN, UPC, barcode, and location terminology
fresh-item record linkage across systems
failure modes from partial or stale identifier evidence

workflow_stages.name: Execution intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm store, department, fixture or case, product family, service window, customer or order record, and tenant authority context before accepting the work and determine whether the request is in scope, out of scope, or blocked.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Fresh-department prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate task list, par sheet, production plan, case or service-counter readiness, inventory state, PLU or GTIN linkage, scale label, date-marking record, temperature log, sanitation evidence, and tenant service policy before execution advances.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior

workflow_stages.name: Order, payment, substitution, refund, and customer-record reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Review POS, OMS, CRM, service desk, pickup or delivery, substitution, refund, and service-recovery records to determine whether customer-facing fresh-department work can safely advance.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Food-safety, traceability, recall, and loss-prevention boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve hold, recall, pull-and-hold, lot-code, illness complaint, foreign-material, shrink, or fraud boundaries whenever these records affect fresh display, production, online order, refund, or service-ticket progression.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and owner routing
workflow_stages.description: Move temperature excursions, date-mark gaps, scale-price conflicts, order mismatches, policy exceptions, customer harm signals, and missing lot evidence into explicit blocked, held, rework, or escalated states with owner routing.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when proof-of-service, proof-of-case readiness, production logs, completion timestamps, customer records, and tenant-specific exception rules have been satisfied and recorded.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior

artifacts: fresh task lists, opening and closing checklists, par sheets, production plans, prep logs, pull sheets, cull logs, trim records, shrink and spoilage logs, and closeout photos
temperature logs, date-marking records, hot-holding and cold-holding records, sanitation logs, shellstock tags, grind logs, and corrective-action notes
POS receipts, price tables, promotion records, scale-label records, PLU, UPC, GTIN, tare, random-weight, unit-price, and markdown records
OMS order states, e-commerce substitution records, pickup or delivery status, customer approval records, CRM notes, service tickets, refund requests, and service-recovery records
inventory states, receiving records, supplier records, lot codes, traceability records, recall notices, pull-and-hold directives, and hold-release notes
food-safety, illness-complaint, foreign-material, fraud, shrink, loss-prevention, and escalation records

allowed: Classify fresh-department requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented execution state and evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed execution status updates, completion records, exception summaries, facilities or workplace service summaries, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
escalate: Required order, payment, refund, pricing, temperature, date-marking, lot, grind, recall, pull-and-hold, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.

The request requires price override, markdown approval, refund exception approval, food-safety release, recall clearance, formal corrective action, fraud adjudication, legal interpretation, or executive approval.
A tenant or jurisdictional pricing, refund, food-safety, traceability, order, recall, or service policy materially conflicts with the public baseline.
Customer harm, fraud, food-safety, foreign-material, illness complaint, recall, or policy exception changes whether execution may proceed.
refuse: Mark work complete without evidence of completion.
Override pricing, refund, food-safety, recall, pull-and-hold, or loss-prevention controls without documented authority.
Release product to sale, pickup, service counter, or hot or cold display when payment, recall, food-safety, lot, fraud, or policy evidence remains unresolved.

domain_failure_modes: fresh cases are marked service-ready even though temperature or date-marking evidence is missing or contradictory
scale labels, unit-price records, shelf signs, and POS prices conflict and the lane improvises a price override
online fresh order, substitution, payment, and customer approval records disagree and product is released anyway
an out-of-policy refund or service-recovery remedy is promised without authorized exception ownership
an illness complaint, foreign-material report, recall, pull-and-hold notice, or lot-code gap is handled as a normal service ticket
meat grind, shellstock, lot, supplier, PLU, UPC, or GTIN evidence is missing and the lane still closes traceback-sensitive work
high-volume fresh-department work is marked complete without department-level readiness and closure evidence
tenant-specific hold, markdown, refund, or food-safety controls are ignored in favor of a generic grocery answer

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

required_workflow_questions_answered.what_booking_order_payment_or_guest_reco: POS receipts, OMS order states, pickup or delivery status, e-commerce substitution records, customer approval records, CRM or service-desk notes, refund or return authorizations, service-recovery records, and customer identifiers govern customer-facing progression. The lane must not rely on department readiness or verbal status when transaction, order, substitution, or customer records disagree.
required_workflow_questions_answered.what_refund_pricing_or_policy_constraint: NIST method-of-sale, unit-price, price-verification, and package-labeling baselines, FTC order and prompt-refund guidance, tenant pricing, markdown, substitution, refund, return, service-recovery, and food-safety policies constrain the workflow. The lane may surface evidence and next steps but may not approve overrides outside delegated limits.
required_workflow_questions_answered.where_does_the_lane_stop_and_pricing_saf: The lane stops when the answer depends on price override authority, markdown or refund exception approval, food-safety release, recall or pull-and-hold clearance, formal corrective action, fraud adjudication, legal interpretation, or executive approval. Those conditions require escalation to the named adjacent specialist or orchestrator return.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific retail food, pricing, and refund rules
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: State or local health department, weights and measures, refund, or consumer-protection rules may be stricter than the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant-supplied jurisdiction and policy overlays at runtime and escalate when they materially change the answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant variation in fresh-department hold, markdown, substitution, and loss-prevention controls
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Retailers vary in when manager approval, lot capture, photo evidence, customer approval, pull-and-hold routing, or LP review is mandatory.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat tenant playbooks and authority matrices as required runtime inputs before closing sensitive cases.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: FSMA traceability implementation timing and tenant readiness
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Public traceability compliance timing and tenant implementation maturity can differ by food category and system readiness.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Use the public source for vocabulary and evidence discipline, require tenant-specific traceability obligations at runtime, and escalate if compliance posture determines the answer.
